# Dev.to Article 2 — The €15,000 VAT Threshold Trap

**Title:** The €15,000 VAT threshold trap catching Portugal's expat freelancers

**Tags:** portugal, freelance, taxes, expat

**Canonical URL:** (none)

---

## Content

Portugal has a €15,000 annual income threshold that most English-speaking freelancers don't hear about until it's too late.

Here's how it catches people:

**What the rule is:**

Freelancers earning under €15,000/year are exempt from charging IVA (VAT) on their invoices — they issue receipts without the 23% VAT line. Above that threshold, they must register for VAT and charge it on every invoice.

The threshold resets annually.

**Why it's a trap:**

The threshold is cumulative across all clients. There's no warning in Portal das Finanças. The system doesn't tell you when you're approaching it. It doesn't block you from issuing receipts without VAT after you've crossed it.

You find out from your accountant — months later, when you're already liable for 23% VAT on invoices you issued (and collected payment on) without the VAT component.

The arithmetic is brutal: if you invoiced €18,000 without VAT after crossing the threshold, you owe ~€690 from your own pocket. You already spent that money.

**Who it hits hardest:**

D8 visa holders in their first full year of freelancing in Portugal. They're earning in a foreign currency, often doing contract work for clients outside Portugal, and the portal doesn't surface this risk in any language — let alone English.

From the Worktugal 2026 guide: *"One wrong selection can accidentally trigger a 23% VAT liability you didn't collect."*

**The fix:**

Track your cumulative annual income against the €15,000 threshold every time you issue a receipt. Set a manual reminder at €12,000 — the point where you need to start planning (registering for VAT takes time).

We built VerdeDesk specifically to surface this. The income dashboard shows your running total against the threshold and flags it when you need to act.

If you're freelancing in Portugal and managing this manually — curious what your current setup looks like.

→ verdedesk.vercel.app
